---
title: "Component A"
---

> **Rename this page** to reflect the actual component (e.g., "Encoder", "Feature Extractor", "Attention Module").

## Role and Responsibility

- **What it does**: [single-sentence description of the transformation performed]
- **Input**: shape, dtype, semantics of the tensor received
- **Output**: shape, dtype, semantics of the tensor produced
- **Invariant**: property that holds regardless of input distribution

```{dot}
//| label: fig-comp-a-role
//| fig-cap: "Component A: data flow"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 2.4
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=LR
  graph [ranksep=0.55, nodesep=0.5]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.55, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  In  [label="Input\n[B × D_in]",  fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  Op1 [label="Op 1"]
  Op2 [label="Op 2"]
  Out [label="Output\n[B × D_out]", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]

  In -> Op1 -> Op2 -> Out
}
```

---

## Internal Structure

- **Layers**: list each sub-layer in order with its output shape
- **Normalisation**: LayerNorm applied [before / after] each block — avoids batch-size sensitivity
- **Activation**: [e.g., GELU / ReLU / SiLU] — justify choice
- **Skip connections**: [yes/no] — describe residual path if applicable

```{dot}
//| label: fig-comp-a-internals
//| fig-cap: "Internal structure with skip connections"
//| fig-width: 6.5
//| fig-height: 3.5
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=TB
  graph [ranksep=0.5, nodesep=0.55]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.55, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph row1 {
    rank=same
    In [label="Input", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }
  subgraph row2 {
    rank=same
    Norm  [label="LayerNorm"]
    Skip1 [label="(identity)", style="dashed", color="#6c757d", fontcolor="#868e96"]
  }
  subgraph row3 {
    rank=same
    Linear [label="Linear"]
    Act    [label="Activation"]
  }
  subgraph row4 {
    rank=same
    Out [label="Output", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
  }

  In -> Norm -> Linear -> Act -> Out
  In -> Skip1 -> Out [style=dashed, color="#6c757d"]
  Norm -> Act
}
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

- **Why LayerNorm over BatchNorm**: stable training with batch size = 1 during inference
- **Why [activation]**: [empirical reason or theoretical justification]
- **Initialisation**: [e.g., Kaiming uniform for linear layers]
- **Alternatives considered**: [alternative A] rejected because [reason]; [alternative B] rejected because [reason]

```{dot}
//| label: fig-comp-a-decisions
//| fig-cap: "Design space: chosen path vs. alternatives"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 2.8
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=LR
  graph [ranksep=0.6, nodesep=0.45]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.55, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph col1 {
    rank=same
    Alt1 [label="Alt A", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
    Chosen [label="Chosen Design", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
    Alt2 [label="Alt B", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
  }
  subgraph col2 {
    rank=same
    Perf [label="Performance"]
    Cost [label="Complexity"]
  }
  subgraph col3 {
    rank=same
    Decision [label="Final Choice", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }

  Alt1   -> Cost [style=dashed, color="#e74c3c"]
  Chosen -> Perf
  Chosen -> Cost
  Alt2   -> Perf [style=dashed, color="#e74c3c"]
  Perf   -> Decision
  Cost   -> Decision
}
```

---

## Configuration

Key parameters exposed through Hydra config (`configs/method/*.yaml`):

| Parameter | Default | Range | Effect |
|-----------|---------|-------|--------|
| `hidden_dim` | — | [32, 512] | capacity vs. overfitting |
| `num_layers` | — | [1, 8] | depth vs. training stability |
| `dropout` | — | [0.0, 0.5] | regularisation strength |

```bash
# Override example
python -m src.main method.hidden_dim=256 method.num_layers=4
```
